goal: Supabase/Postgres-backed multi-organization Orun SaaS control plane
next_focus: PERF connection-reuse (task 0134) REVERTED — #224/#225 shipped a module-scoped postgres client pool but stage verification FAILED: Cloudflare Workers rejects reusing a client/socket opened in another request ('Cannot perform I/O on behalf of a different request'). membership-worker returned 500 and billing went flaky 503; the self-heal retry did not recover. Rolled back via PR #227 (squash 2f5f4fc): executor.ts restored to per-request client creation + real dispose() (with a comment documenting why reuse is unsafe in Workers), removed the module pool + __poolTestHooks + pool test, re-touched all 10 DB-using workers to redeploy the safe executor (Orun doesn't cascade packages/db changes). A Workers-safe reuse approach (Hyperdrive driver guidance / ctx.waitUntil(sql.end())) would need a canary before any retry. Post-#227 stage verification: billing/summary recovered to HTTP 200 (10/10) but members stayed a hard 500 — root-caused to a SEPARATE pre-existing bug from PERF3 (#221), not connection reuse: the batched role-assignment lookup used `subject_id = ANY($2)` with a JS array param, which throws at bind time because the pg driver runs `fetch_types:false` (no array element-type OID). Fixed by switching to a scalar parameterized IN-list (still one batched query, N+1 win preserved); regression test added; membership-worker re-touched to redeploy. NEXT: confirm members HTTP 200 on stage after that deploy, then 0133 PERF4 (hot-path hop reduction + parallelization + Server-Timing observability). Remaining PERF after that: reverse-lookup index migration; Hyperdrive cache-eligibility audit. PRIOR DONE: 0130 (client cache), 0131 (edge bearer cache), 0132 (parallel billing + member N+1).
notes: 2026-06-01: ai/ working tree compacted. Historical task prompts (ai/tasks/), implementer/verifier reports (ai/reports/), spec proposals (ai/proposals/), the task ledger (ai/context/task-ledger.md), and the orchestrator journal/brief were removed from the working tree to keep a minimal context surface. ALL remain recoverable from git history (see ai/context/current.md for retrieval). Live boot surface kept: this file, ai/context/current.md (compact summary), ai/context/decisions.md, ai/context/open-risks.md, ai/deferred.md, ai/waiting_for_input.md.

2026-06-01: Task 0126 (B5 manual webhook delivery replay) DONE — implemented and merged as PR #181, squash 63886a6, now main HEAD. State files previously lagged reality (said 0126 only scoped). No active task; orchestrator must re-survey to scope the next leg.

2026-06-02: Task 0134 (connection reuse) REVERTED. The module-scoped postgres client pool (#224/#225) broke on stage — Cloudflare Workers cannot reuse a socket opened in a different request ('Cannot perform I/O on behalf of a different request'); membership 500, billing flaky 503, self-heal retry insufficient. Rolled back via PR #227 (squash 2f5f4fc) to per-request executor + dispose(). Lesson: any cross-request connection reuse on Workers must go through Hyperdrive-supported patterns and be canaried before retry.

2026-06-02: While verifying the 0134 revert on stage, found members list returning a hard 500 (billing recovered fine). Root cause was NOT connection reuse but a PERF3 (#221) regression: `membership.role_assignments ... subject_id = ANY($2)` passed a JS array param, which fails to bind because the executor uses `fetch_types:false` (postgres can't resolve the array element-type OID). Fixed in packages/db by using a scalar parameterized IN-list; the connection-reuse stage test had misattributed this to its own change. Lesson: array params are unsafe with fetch_types:false — prefer scalar IN-lists; and verify ALL DB-backed endpoints (not just billing/orgs) after perf changes.
